channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Havana slams new Trump sanctions as ‘collective punishment’ of Cuban people
item.description: The Cuban government has firmly rejected the recent sanctions levied by US President Donald Trump.

item.title: Peru probes trafficking of citizens to fight for Russia in Ukraine
item.description: Peru's public prosecutor says many of their citizens are victims of deception, lured by ⁠the promise of jobs.

item.title: K-pop’s BTS comeback tour rallies South Korea’s global ‘soft power’ drive
item.description: K-pop sensations BTS ignited a wave of interest in South Korean culture that now extends from food to cosmetics.

item.title: Iran war live: Trump says no ‘early’ end to war, unhappy with Tehran offer
item.description: White House tells Congress that Iran hostilities have 'terminated' despite continued US troop presence in Middle East.

item.title: US said to be withdrawing 5,000 troops from Germany over Iran war spat
item.description: President Donald Trump has feuded with European allies over their reluctance to step up support for war on Iran.

item.title: Trump considering option to ‘blast the hell out of’ Iran
item.description: US President Donald Trump said he is "not happy" with the latest peace proposal from Iran.

item.title: Trump expands red snapper fishing as critics warn of overfishing
item.description: Anglers in Florida, Georgia, and Carolinas gain more fishing control with Trump's red snapper permit approval.

item.title: At least 12 killed in latest Israeli attacks on Lebanon
item.description: Israel's strikes continue, with more than 2,600 killed since March 2, despite an ongoing ceasefire.

item.title: Iranian Nobel laureate Narges Mohammadi hospitalised as health deteriorates
item.description: Mohammadi has lost consciousness twice and suffered a severe cardiac crisis, her foundation has announced.

item.title: May Day rallies sweep US, demanding reforms for working-class rights
item.description: May Day events include calls to 'tax the rich' and abolish ICE amid $70bn proposed funding for immigration enforcement.

item.title: Palestinian football boss refuses Israeli handshake at FIFA event
item.description: Palestine’s FA President, Jibril Rajoub, explained his refusal to shake hands with Israeli FA representative.

item.title: Cuban man is 18th person to die in US ICE custody this year: Monitor
item.description: Rights group says believed suicide underscores need for more oversight amid Trump's mass deportation drive.

item.title: Barcelona in sight of La Liga title a week before El Clasico with Real
item.description: Barcelona face Osasuna in La Liga on Saturday where a win will heap more pressure on Real Madrid at Espanyol on Sunday.

item.title: US judge bars Trump from ending protected status for Yemeni nationals
item.description: Trump administration has sought to cancel temporary protections for 13 countries as part of immigration crackdown.

item.title: Rebel checkpoints reported around Mali’s capital, northern town seized
item.description: JNIM and Tuareg separatists continue their attacks against Mali's military government.

item.title: Photos: Cuba holds May Day celebrations amid US threats
item.description: March celebrating International Workers' Day coincided with announcement of further US sanctions on island.

item.title: Trump says Iran seeks terms he ‘can’t agree to’ in latest peace proposal
item.description: Trump says the Iranian offer sent via Pakistan contains terms he's not satisfied with.

item.title: Trump announces 25 percent tariffs on European Union cars, trucks
item.description: US president says EU ‘not complying’ with current trade deal, launches new tariffs to begin next week.

item.title: Poll finds 61 percent of Americans believe attacking Iran was a mistake
item.description: Poll finds that Americans are concerned about impact of the war on the cost of living and sceptical of success thus far.

item.title: US warns shippers against paying Strait of Hormuz tolls, ‘donations’
item.description: Latest warning comes as Iranian state media reports Tehran has presented new peace proposal to US.

item.title: Michael Jackson’s biopic and what it leaves out
item.description: A new biopic revisits Michael Jackson - but what’s left out of his story, and who decides his legacy?

item.title: UAE exit from OPEC signals closer alignment with US interests, experts say
item.description: UAE wants to supply more oil than its OPEC quota and that could help push down prices once the Strait of Hormuz opens.

item.title: EU trade deal with South America’s Mercosur bloc takes provisional effect
item.description: The long-awaited deal creates one of the world’s largest free trade areas with 720m potential consumers.

item.title: Pentagon announces deal with seven AI companies for classified systems
item.description: Most recent instance of department integrating with AI comes amid Anthropic standoff, concern over use in Iran war.

item.title: Turkish police fire tear gas, arrest hundreds at May Day rally
item.description: Turkish authorities violently crackdown on May Day demonstrations at Istanbul’s Taksim Square, firing tear gas.
